fields.name: planning_effort
fields.description: Auto-config planning-effort tier (spec 2026-06-06 §Phase 0). Controls how hard the controller searches: 'fast' = a single cheap pass; 'normal' (default) = today's interactive budget; 'thinking'/'einstein' spend the freed engine cycles on a larger sample, more refit iterations, and — at thinking+ — measuring real blocking on the full frame instead of extrapolating. Overridable via the GOLDENMATCH_PLANNING_EFFORT env var. Default 'normal' is byte-for-byte the prior behavior.

fields.name: throughput
fields.description: Opt-in sketch-then-verify throughput tier for high-recall, low-cost dedup.

fields.name: memory
fields.description: Learning Memory configuration for persisting corrections and learned thresholds.

fields.name: identity
fields.description: Identity Graph configuration for stable cross-run entity ids.

fields.name: exclude_columns
fields.description: Column names to skip across the suite. GoldenMatch auto-config never picks these for matchkeys/blocking. GoldenFlow transforms skip them entirely (column passes through unchanged). Layered ADDITIVELY with GoldenCheck detector-derived exclusions (#404) -- the user list is OR'd with auto-detection, not a replacement. `QualityConfig.autoconfig_force_include` still wins on conflict (rescue beats every opt-out path). Column still appears in golden record output -- exclusion is about matching + transforming, not output. See spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-21-unified-column-exclusions-design.md.

fields.name: prepared_record_store
fields.description: When True, the prep stage (quality scan + transform + auto-fix) writes its output to a DuckDB-backed disk store keyed by config signature. Subsequent calls with the same config + data shape read prepared records from disk instead of re-prepping. Path via GOLDENMATCH_PREPARED_RECORD_STORE_DIR env var; persistence via GOLDENMATCH_PREPARED_RECORD_STORE_PERSIST=1. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-15-distributed-plan-v1-design.md §Component 1.

fields.name: partitioned_block_scoring
fields.description: When True AND prepared_record_store is True, the pipeline materializes blocks to the disk store as a side effect of build_blocks (Component 2 Phase 2 of Distributed Plan v1). Stages on-disk blocks for Component 3 (distributed scoring); no single-process win expected. Default off.

fields.name: n_buckets
fields.description: Number of hash buckets for Component 2 v2 bucketed Parquet storage. None means use the heuristic default max(cpu_count() * 4, 64). Hard-capped at 1024. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-17-distributed-plan-component-2-v2-bucketed-storage-design.md §Configuration.

fields.name: channel_column

fields.name: adjust_cross_channel

fields.name: prob_threshold

mcp_tools.name: add_correction
mcp_tools.description: Add a Learning Memory correction. Two shapes:

mcp_tools.name: agent_approve_reject
mcp_tools.description: Approve or reject a review queue pair

mcp_tools.name: agent_compare_strategies
mcp_tools.description: Compare ER strategies on your data

mcp_tools.name: agent_deduplicate
mcp_tools.description: Deduplicate ONE dataset: auto-configures matching, runs the full entity-resolution pipeline, and returns the duplicate clusters with confidence gating (auto-merge / needs-review / reject) plus per-decision reasoning. Takes a file path or an uploaded dataset; pass output_path to also write the golden (deduplicated) records to CSV. Operates on a single source -- to link records across TWO datasets use agent_match_sources instead.

mcp_tools.name: agent_explain_cluster
mcp_tools.description: Explain why records are in the same cluster

mcp_tools.name: agent_explain_pair
mcp_tools.description: Natural language explanation for a record pair

mcp_tools.name: agent_match_sources
mcp_tools.description: Link records across TWO datasets (not dedupe one): auto-selects a matching strategy, scores cross-source pairs, and returns the matched pairs with confidence. Takes two file paths or uploaded datasets (file_a / file_b); pass output_path to also write the matched pairs to CSV. To find duplicates WITHIN a single dataset use agent_deduplicate instead.

mcp_tools.name: agent_review_queue
mcp_tools.description: Get borderline pairs awaiting approval

mcp_tools.name: analyze_blocking
mcp_tools.description: Diagnose blocking on the loaded dataset: returns ranked blocking key candidates with block counts, max block size, total candidate comparisons, and estimated recall. Use it to explain why matching is slow or produces too many candidate pairs.

mcp_tools.name: analyze_data
mcp_tools.description: Profile a dataset (read-only, runs no matching and writes nothing): column stats, the detected domain/entity type, data-quality signals, and a recommended entity-resolution strategy. Takes a file path or an uploaded dataset. Use it before deduplicating to understand the data and preview the strategy the auto-config would pick.

mcp_tools.name: auto_configure
mcp_tools.description: Run AutoConfigController on a CSV; return the committed GoldenMatchConfig (incl. negative_evidence / Path Y when chosen) plus telemetry — stop_reason, health, decision trace, indicator column priors. Programmatic equivalent of `goldenmatch autoconfig`.

mcp_tools.name: certify_recall
mcp_tools.description: Estimate match RECALL without ground truth (unsupervised). Treats each auto-configured matchkey/pass as a decorrelated system and uses capture-recapture over their overlaps to estimate how many true matches were missed. Returns a point estimate (a safe lower bound additionally needs a small labelled audit; see `goldenmatch evaluate --certify --audit-out`). Needs >=3 decorrelated systems.

mcp_tools.name: compare_clusters
mcp_tools.description: Compare two ER clustering outcomes on the same dataset without ground truth (CCMS): classifies each cluster as unchanged / merged / partitioned / overlapping and returns the Talburt-Wang Index. Both inputs are JSON cluster files (as written by export-style output).

mcp_tools.name: config_weaknesses
mcp_tools.description: Diagnose weaknesses in the loaded run's auto-config: columns admitted that shouldn't be (source/provenance labels, per-row IDs), oversized or shared-value blocks, null sinks, low-signal matchkeys, and over-merging. Returns ranked findings, each with a plain-English explanation + a concrete fix, plus a one-paragraph summary.

mcp_tools.name: controller_telemetry
mcp_tools.description: Return the AutoConfigController telemetry from the most recent `auto_configure` or `agent_deduplicate` call in this MCP session. Same JSON shape as the web /api/v1/controller/telemetry endpoint.

mcp_tools.name: convert_splink_config
mcp_tools.description: Convert a Splink settings JSON (bare or trained) into a GoldenMatch config. Pass the settings as an inline JSON string -- no filesystem access needed. Returns the config as YAML, a findings report (severity/splink_path/message/mapped_to per finding), a summary, and -- when the Splink input carried trained m/u probabilities -- the imported EM model as a dict you can persist yourself. strict=True fails on ANY lossy mapping (not just hard errors).

mcp_tools.name: create_domain
mcp_tools.description: Create a custom domain extraction rulebook. Define patterns for a specific data domain (medical devices, automotive parts, real estate, etc.).

mcp_tools.name: dedupe
mcp_tools.description: Alias for `find_duplicates`. Find duplicate matches for a record. Provide field values to search against the loaded dataset.

mcp_tools.name: documents_ingest
mcp_tools.description: Extract records from documents (PDF/image) against a target schema into rows ready for dedupe_df. Returns records + an ingest report.

mcp_tools.name: documents_suggest_schema
mcp_tools.description: Propose a target extraction schema (JSON) from a sample document image/PDF.

mcp_tools.name: evaluate
mcp_tools.description: Score the loaded run against ground-truth pairs. Loads a ground-truth CSV (id_a,id_b columns) and returns precision, recall, and F1 for the current clustering.

mcp_tools.name: explain_cluster
mcp_tools.description: Alias for `agent_explain_cluster`. Explain why records are in the same cluster

mcp_tools.name: explain_match
mcp_tools.description: Explain why two records match or don't match. Shows per-field score breakdown.

mcp_tools.name: explain_pair
mcp_tools.description: Alias for `explain_match`. Explain why two records match or don't match. Shows per-field score breakdown.

mcp_tools.name: explain_routing
mcp_tools.description: Human-readable explanation of why each stage is routed the way it is, with the driver-RAM projection that drove it.

mcp_tools.name: export_results
mcp_tools.description: Export matching results to a file (CSV or JSON).

mcp_tools.name: find_duplicates
mcp_tools.description: Find duplicate matches for a record. Provide field values to search against the loaded dataset.

mcp_tools.name: fix_quality
mcp_tools.description: Run GoldenCheck scan and apply fixes to a CSV file. Returns the fixed data summary and a manifest of all fixes applied. Requires goldencheck: pip install goldenmatch[quality]

mcp_tools.name: get_cluster
mcp_tools.description: Get details of a specific cluster: all member records and their field values.

mcp_tools.name: get_golden_record
mcp_tools.description: Get the merged golden (canonical) record for a cluster.

mcp_tools.name: get_stats
mcp_tools.description: Get dataset statistics: record count, cluster count, match rate, cluster sizes.

mcp_tools.name: identity_audit
mcp_tools.description: Export the append-only identity audit log in commit order: every event with actor / trust / timestamp / reason, so a reviewer can reconstruct exactly which actor changed what, when, and why. Optionally filtered by dataset / actor.

mcp_tools.name: identity_audit_seal
mcp_tools.description: Anchor the append-only audit log with a tamper-evidence seal: a chained sha256 root over every event since the last seal. Cheap and idempotent (a no-op when nothing new has been logged). Run it periodically (or after a batch of stewardship actions) so the history becomes provably untampered. Optionally scoped to a dataset. Publish/mirror the returned root_hash to make tampering detectable by an external party.

mcp_tools.name: identity_audit_verify
mcp_tools.description: Verify the append-only audit log against its seal chain. Replays the per-event content hashes and the seal roots to detect content edits, deletion, reordering, and insertion of any sealed event. Returns {ok, events_checked, seals_checked} plus the ids of any content mismatches / broken seals / missing sealed events. Optionally scoped to a dataset.

mcp_tools.name: identity_claim
mcp_tools.description: Claim a record into an identity, moving it out of any prior entity ('this record belongs to that identity'). Emits a provenance-stamped `claimed` event on both the gaining and losing entities.

mcp_tools.name: identity_conflicts
mcp_tools.description: List evidence edges marked `conflicts_with`.

mcp_tools.name: identity_history
mcp_tools.description: Return the temporal event log for an identity.

mcp_tools.name: identity_list
mcp_tools.description: List identities, optionally filtered by dataset/status.

mcp_tools.name: identity_merge
mcp_tools.description: Manually merge two identities. All records from `absorb_entity_id` are reassigned to `keep_entity_id`. The merge events are stamped with `actor`/`trust` provenance so the audit log records who merged these and on what authority.

mcp_tools.name: identity_profile
mcp_tools.description: MDM profile of one entity: record count + per-source breakdown, golden record, confidence, conflict count, canonical version (structural-event count), and first/last activity. Returns {found: false} when no such entity exists.

mcp_tools.name: identity_resolve
mcp_tools.description: Resolve a record_id to its durable identity. Returns the full identity view (members, evidence edges, recent events) or null when no identity exists for that record.

mcp_tools.name: identity_resolve_conflict
mcp_tools.description: Adjudicate a `conflicts_with` pair: 'same' keeps the entity intact, 'distinct' splits the second record out into a new identity, 'defer' only logs. Records a durable mediation verdict + event with actor/trust provenance, and stops the conflict re-surfacing in the open-conflicts queue.

mcp_tools.name: identity_show
mcp_tools.description: Fetch the full detail of one identity by entity_id: its member records, evidence edges, and recent event log. Returns {found: false} when no such entity exists.

mcp_tools.name: identity_split
mcp_tools.description: Split a subset of records off an identity into a brand-new identity. The original keeps the remaining records. The split events carry `actor`/`trust` provenance.

mcp_tools.name: identity_stats
mcp_tools.description: Graph-level summary / health stats: entities by status, total records, records-per-entity distribution, conflict total, source mix, and the largest entities. Optionally scoped to a dataset.

mcp_tools.name: identity_worklist
mcp_tools.description: Prioritized steward worklist: active entities needing attention (open conflicts and/or confidence below weak_confidence), highest conflict count first.

mcp_tools.name: incremental
mcp_tools.description: Match a batch of new records against an existing base dataset (without re-running the whole base). Returns matched (new_row_id, base_row_id, score) pairs plus counts. Auto-configures from the base file if no config is given.

mcp_tools.name: learn_thresholds
mcp_tools.description: Force a MemoryLearner pass over accumulated corrections. Returns the list of LearnedAdjustments produced (matchkey_name, threshold, sample_size, learned_at). Requires >= 10 corrections per matchkey before threshold tuning fires; otherwise returns an empty list.

mcp_tools.name: lineage
mcp_tools.description: Field-level provenance for the loaded run: for each scored pair, the per-field scores that produced the match, plus cluster id. Optionally write a lineage JSON to a directory.

mcp_tools.name: lint_routing
mcp_tools.description: Flag config/env overrides that force a slow path (e.g. CLUSTERING_THRESHOLD=0 when the edge set fits driver RAM). ERROR at scale; would_refuse mirrors the runtime guard.

mcp_tools.name: list_blocking_strategies
mcp_tools.description: List all blocking strategy names.

mcp_tools.name: list_clusters
mcp_tools.description: List duplicate clusters found in the dataset. Returns cluster IDs, sizes, and member counts.

mcp_tools.name: list_corrections
mcp_tools.description: List stored Learning Memory corrections, optionally filtered by dataset. Returns id_a, id_b, decision, source, trust, reason, matchkey_name, dataset, original_score, created_at.

mcp_tools.name: list_domains
mcp_tools.description: List available domain extraction rulebooks (built-in + user-defined).

mcp_tools.name: list_plugins
mcp_tools.description: List all registered goldenmatch plugins by category. Includes the 22 v1.18.2 predefined plugins (numeric/format/business/aggregation) plus any user-registered plugins via entry-points or PluginRegistry.register_*(). Each entry includes name, source (builtin or user), category, and the first line of the merge docstring.

mcp_tools.name: list_runs
mcp_tools.description: List previous dedupe/match runs (for rollback) from the run log.

mcp_tools.name: list_scorers
mcp_tools.description: List all available similarity scorers.

mcp_tools.name: list_strategies
mcp_tools.description: List all golden-record survivorship strategies.

mcp_tools.name: list_transforms
mcp_tools.description: List all available field transforms.

mcp_tools.name: match
mcp_tools.description: Alias for `match_record`. Match a single record against the loaded dataset in real-time. Paste a record's fields and instantly see if it matches any existing record. Uses the configured matchkeys, scorers, and thresholds. Example: {"name": "John Smith", "email": "john@test.com", "zip": "10001"}

mcp_tools.name: match_record
mcp_tools.description: Match a single record against the loaded dataset in real-time. Paste a record's fields and instantly see if it matches any existing record. Uses the configured matchkeys, scorers, and thresholds. Example: {"name": "John Smith", "email": "john@test.com", "zip": "10001"}

mcp_tools.name: memory_export
mcp_tools.description: Return all corrections as a list of dicts (CSV-shaped). Caller is responsible for writing the file. Optionally filter by dataset.

mcp_tools.name: memory_import
mcp_tools.description: Import corrections from a list of dicts (the exact shape memory_export returns). Upserts into the store: higher trust wins, same trust = latest wins. Returns the count imported.

mcp_tools.name: memory_stats
mcp_tools.description: Return Learning Memory status: total correction count, last learn time, and current learned adjustments. Cheap; safe for status checks.

mcp_tools.name: plan_routing
mcp_tools.description: Project per-stage distributed routing (scoring/clustering/golden) for a given data shape + cluster. Pure; no controller run.

mcp_tools.name: pprl_auto_config
mcp_tools.description: Analyze the loaded dataset and recommend optimal PPRL (privacy-preserving record linkage) configuration. Returns recommended fields, bloom filter parameters, threshold, and explanation.

mcp_tools.name: pprl_link
mcp_tools.description: Run privacy-preserving record linkage between two parties' data. Computes bloom filters, matches records without sharing raw data. Specify fields, threshold, and security level.

mcp_tools.name: profile
mcp_tools.description: Alias for `profile_data`. Get data quality profile: column types, null rates, unique counts, sample values.

mcp_tools.name: profile_data
mcp_tools.description: Get data quality profile: column types, null rates, unique counts, sample values.

mcp_tools.name: retrieve_similar
mcp_tools.description: Semantic retrieval (#1089): return the records in a CSV most similar to a free-text query, ranked by cosine similarity. Embeds the chosen column and the query with the zero-config in-house embedder (no cloud/torch by default) and runs ANN search. The read side of the RAG entity-canonicalization epic -- fetch candidate records by query without running a full dedupe.

mcp_tools.name: review_config
mcp_tools.description: Run the config healer over the loaded dataset: analyze the dedupe run and return ranked, self-verified suggestions for improving the matching config (thresholds, scorers, negative evidence, blocking). Each suggestion carries an id, kind, target, rationale, and a machine-applicable patch. Requires the native kernel (pip install goldenmatch[native]); returns an empty list otherwise.

mcp_tools.name: rollback
mcp_tools.description: Undo a previous run by DELETING its output files (looked up by run_id in the run log). Destructive: removes the files that run wrote. Use list_runs first to find the run_id.

mcp_tools.name: run_transforms
mcp_tools.description: Run GoldenFlow data transforms on a CSV file. Normalizes phone numbers (E.164), dates (ISO), categorical spelling, and Unicode issues. Returns a manifest of transforms applied. Requires goldenflow: pip install goldenmatch[transform]

mcp_tools.name: scan_quality
mcp_tools.description: Run GoldenCheck data quality scan on a CSV file. Returns issues found (encoding errors, Unicode problems, format violations) without applying fixes. Requires goldencheck: pip install goldenmatch[quality]

mcp_tools.name: schema_match
mcp_tools.description: Auto-map columns between two files with different schemas. Returns proposed (col_a, col_b) mappings with a confidence score and method (synonym / name_sim / composite). Useful before matching two sources.

mcp_tools.name: sensitivity
mcp_tools.description: Parameter-sensitivity analysis: sweep one or more config parameters across a range and report how stable the clustering is at each value (CCMS unchanged %). Use it to find robust thresholds. Auto-configures the file if no config is given.

mcp_tools.name: shatter_cluster
mcp_tools.description: Break an entire cluster into individual records. All members become singletons. Use when a cluster is completely wrong.

mcp_tools.name: suggest_config
mcp_tools.description: Analyze bad merges and suggest config changes. Provide examples of incorrect merges (pairs that should NOT have matched) and GoldenMatch will identify which fields/thresholds to tighten. Example: [{"record_a": {...}, "record_b": {...}, "reason": "different people"}]

mcp_tools.name: suggest_pprl
mcp_tools.description: Check if data needs privacy-preserving matching

mcp_tools.name: test_domain
mcp_tools.description: Test a domain extraction rulebook against sample records. Shows what features would be extracted from the loaded data.

mcp_tools.name: unmerge_record
mcp_tools.description: Remove a record from its cluster. The record becomes a singleton. Remaining cluster members are re-clustered using stored pair scores. Use this to fix bad merges.

mcp_tools.name: upload_dataset
mcp_tools.description: Upload a local file's bytes to the server and get back a server-side path to reuse across other tools (analyze_data, auto_configure, agent_deduplicate, ...). No hosting needed. Send base64 (default) or raw text via `encoding`. Uploaded files are ephemeral scratch, reaped after GOLDENMATCH_MCP_UPLOAD_TTL (default 24h); re-upload if you need a path older than that. Max size GOLDENMATCH_MCP_MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 64MB) -- above it, pass a public http(s) URL as file_path instead.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Schema version of this configuration file, used to handle format changes across releases.

fields.name: settings
fields.description: Global run settings such as sampling size and severity handling that apply to the whole check.

fields.name: columns
fields.description: Per-column validation rules keyed by column name.

fields.name: relations
fields.description: Cross-column relationship rules applied across multiple columns.

fields.name: ignore
fields.description: List of column and check pairs whose findings should be excluded from results.

fields.name: sample_size
fields.description: Maximum number of rows sampled from the dataset when running checks, capping work on large tables.

fields.name: severity_threshold
fields.description: Minimum severity level at which a finding is reported, so anything below this floor is suppressed from results.

fields.name: fail_on
fields.description: Severity level that causes the overall run to fail once a finding at or above it is produced.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Expected data type for the column, used to flag values that do not conform to it.

fields.name: required
fields.description: Whether the column must be present in the dataset, failing the check when it is missing.

fields.name: nullable
fields.description: Whether null values are permitted in the column, flagging nulls when set to false.

fields.name: format
fields.description: Named format the column values must match, such as an email or date pattern, for validating string shape.

fields.name: unique
fields.description: Whether every value in the column must be distinct, flagging duplicate values when set to true.

fields.name: range
fields.description: Inclusive lower and upper numeric bounds that column values must fall within.

fields.name: enum
fields.description: Closed set of allowed values for the column, flagging any value outside this list.

fields.name: outlier_stddev
fields.description: Number of standard deviations from the mean beyond which a numeric value is flagged as an outlier.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Kind of cross-column relationship to enforce, for example a uniqueness or foreign-key style constraint.

fields.name: columns
fields.description: Columns that participate in the relationship, evaluated together as a group.

fields.name: column
fields.description: Column whose check results should be suppressed from the report.

fields.name: check
fields.description: Specific check on that column to skip, silencing its findings for that column.

mcp_tools.name: analyze_data
mcp_tools.description: Analyze a data file to detect its domain, profile columns, and recommend a scanning strategy. Returns domain detection, column count, row count, strategy decisions, and alternative approaches.

mcp_tools.name: approve_reject
mcp_tools.description: Approve (pin) or reject (dismiss) a review queue item. Decision must be 'pin' or 'dismiss'.

mcp_tools.name: auto_configure
mcp_tools.description: Scan a data file, triage findings by confidence, and generate goldencheck.yml content from the pinned findings. Optionally accepts constraints to filter or adjust the generated config.

mcp_tools.name: compare_domains
mcp_tools.description: Scan a file with every available domain pack (plus base/no-domain) and compare health scores. Recommends the best-fitting domain.

mcp_tools.name: explain_column
mcp_tools.description: Get a natural-language health narrative for a specific column. Scans the file, profiles the column, and explains all findings.

mcp_tools.name: explain_finding
mcp_tools.description: Explain a single finding in natural language. Requires the finding as a JSON dict and the file_path to load a profile for context.

mcp_tools.name: get_column_detail
mcp_tools.description: Get detailed profile and findings for a specific column.

mcp_tools.name: get_domain_info
mcp_tools.description: Get detailed info about a specific domain pack — lists all semantic types, their name hints, and suppression rules.

mcp_tools.name: health_score
mcp_tools.description: Get the health score (A-F, 0-100) for a data file. Quick summary of overall data quality.

mcp_tools.name: install_domain
mcp_tools.description: Download a community domain pack from the goldencheck-types repository and save it for use in future scans.

mcp_tools.name: list_checks
mcp_tools.description: List all available profiler checks and what they detect. No arguments needed.

mcp_tools.name: list_domains
mcp_tools.description: List all available domain packs (healthcare, finance, ecommerce, etc.). Domain packs provide specialized semantic type definitions for specific data domains.

mcp_tools.name: pipeline_handoff
mcp_tools.description: Generate a structured quality attestation JSON for a data file. Includes health score, findings summary, pinned rules, and attestation status (PASS, PASS_WITH_WARNINGS, REVIEW_REQUIRED, FAIL).

mcp_tools.name: profile
mcp_tools.description: Profile a data file and return column-level statistics: type, null%, unique%, min/max, top values, detected formats. Also returns a health score (A-F) based on finding severity.

mcp_tools.name: review_queue
mcp_tools.description: List all pending review items for a given job. Returns items that need human decision (medium-confidence findings).

mcp_tools.name: review_stats
mcp_tools.description: Get review queue statistics for a job — counts of pending, pinned, and dismissed items.

mcp_tools.name: scan
mcp_tools.description: Scan a data file (CSV, Parquet, Excel) for data quality issues. Returns findings with severity, confidence, affected rows, and sample values. No configuration needed — rules are discovered from the data.

mcp_tools.name: suggest_fix
mcp_tools.description: Preview fixes for a data file without applying them. Shows what would change (columns, fix types, rows affected, before/after samples).

mcp_tools.name: validate
mcp_tools.description: Validate a data file against pinned rules in goldencheck.yml. Returns validation findings (existence, required, unique, enum, range checks).

mcp_tools.name: explain_transform

mcp_tools.name: learn

mcp_tools.name: list_domains
mcp_tools.description: List available domain packs (e.g., people_hr, ecommerce, finance).

mcp_tools.name: map

mcp_tools.name: profile

mcp_tools.name: select_from_findings

mcp_tools.name: transform
